ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza class (FOODON_00002572); no specific class exists for fish-topped pizza. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers pizza as a category; no specific fish bits pizza sub-code exists. Closest parent match used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for pizza or fish pizza dish type.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium 8-inch fish bits pizza weighing approximately 400 g, typically yielding 4–6 slices and representing a single-person meal serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with Meat and Vegetables (SR Legacy) and Finfish, Mixed Species, Cooked, Dry Heat (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for fish bits pizza. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for meat/cheese pizza (FDC ID 170919) and cooked fish (FDC ID 175168), weighted to reflect the approximate ingredient composition of a fish-topped pizza with mozzarella and tomato sauce. Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against these USDA references and retained where consistent. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and water data are set to -1 as detailed values for this composite dish are not directly reported in USDA SR Legacy for this specific combination.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for fish bits pizza. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: standard cheese pizza on refined wheat crust (GI ~60, University of Sydney GI Database). The deep-fried fish topping adds fat which modestly slows gastric emptying but does not substantially alter the GI of the refined wheat base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × available carbohydrate per serving (85 g from 400 g × 0.2125) / 100 = 51.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 450 mg/100 g; a full 400 g serving delivers approximately 1800 mg sodium, representing 78–90% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit fish bits pizza to one or two slices per occasion, request reduced cheese and lighter tomato sauce application, and avoid additional high-sodium condiments to keep total meal sodium below 600 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat pizza crust with a medium GI of approximately 60 and a high glycemic load of 51 per full serving; low dietary fibre at 1.25 g/100 g provides insufficient buffering of postprandial glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict portions to one or two slices, request a thin whole-wheat crust to increase fibre and reduce glycemic load, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 5.0 g/100 g from mozzarella and frying oil, trans fat at 0.13 g/100 g from deep-frying, and high sodium at 450 mg/100 g collectively elevate cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional small portions, request baked rather than deep-fried fish to reduce saturated and trans fat, and choose reduced-fat cheese options to lower saturated fat intake per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; saturated fat at 5.0 g/100 g and nightshade-derived tomato sauce may exacerbate inflammatory burden.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request baked fish topping instead of deep-fried, reduce tomato sauce quantity, and limit portion size to minimise AGE and saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Bits Pizza is a Singaporean fusion dish featuring crispy deep-fried white fish pieces scattered over a mozzarella cheese base and tomato sauce on a standard or naan-style pizza crust, then baked until the cheese is melted and the crust is golden. The fish pieces are typically coated in a seasoned batter before deep-frying, yielding a crunchy exterior that contrasts with the soft, chewy pizza base. The flavour profile combines the savoury umami of mozzarella and tomato sauce with the mild sweetness of white fish and the richness of fried batter. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 262.5 kcal, 13.75 g protein, 13.75 g fat (of which 5.0 g is saturated), 21.25 g carbohydrate, 1.25 g dietary fibre, and 450 mg sodium. The medium glycemic index of approximately 60 and high glycemic load per full serving make this dish a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. The deep-fried preparation method generates advanced glycation end-products relevant to inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis. This dish is commonly available through food delivery platforms in Singapore and is suited to lunch or dinner occasions. It is not suitable for vegetarian, vegan, or gluten-free diets, and contains fish, dairy, and gluten allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in fish bits pizza?
Is fish pizza high in sodium?
What are the ingredients in fish bits pizza?
Is fish bits pizza suitable for hypertension?
How much protein in fish pizza?
What is the glycemic index of fish pizza?

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for fish bits pizza as a named dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for cheese pizza and cooked fish, cross-checked against the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7). The GI is an analogous estimate based on standard cheese pizza. Confidence is medium due to the composite nature of the dish and absence of a direct primary-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for this composite fish bits pizza dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. Input cultural tags (Mexican, Taco, Burrito) were corrected to Singaporean Fusion as the dish originates in Singapore and is sold via Singaporean food delivery platforms; the Mexican ethnic tags in the source record appear to be a data error. The input primary_cuisine field listed 'Mexican' which is inconsistent with the dish description and country_of_origin 'Singapore'; this has been corrected to Singaporean Fusion. Halal status is assumed based on the use of fish and standard pizza ingredients without pork-derived components, but should be verified against the specific vendor's certification.
